query:
INSERT INTO public.questions (
    id, question_text, question_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint,
    stimulus_text, stimulus_type, section, topic, difficulty, exam_type,
    is_platform_question, ai_generated, calculator_allowed, subject, subtopic,
    math_domain, pool_key, diagnostic_eligible, tags, external_id, source, source_metadata
  ) VALUES
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following best describes the main idea of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"America''s history of racial injustice is a complex and difficult issue.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Black Americans have a long history of fighting for equality and freedom.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"To achieve true change, one must confront and acknowledge past injustices.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The future of America depends on the willingness of its people to confront the past.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The passage centers on Baldwin''s belief that confronting and acknowledging the past is crucial for achieving change. The quote "Not everything that is faced can be changed. But nothing can be changed until it is faced" directly supports this idea.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In his acclaimed 1965 book "The Fire Next Time," James Baldwin argues that, given the history of racial injustice in America, it is impossible for Black people to achieve genuine equality and freedom without a full reckoning with the past. He writes, "Not everything that is faced can be changed. But nothing can be changed until it is faced."',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    '2376c971_english_00675',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"2376c971","opensat_index":675}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The writer most likely chose to end the paragraph with that line in order to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"create a sense of mystery and suspense.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"emphasize the beauty of the setting.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"introduce the theme of journey.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"show the reader’s perspective.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The imagery of the "ribbon of black asphalt snaking through the moonlit pines" creates a vivid and evocative picture of the setting, emphasizing its beauty and natural appeal. This helps to set the tone and mood for the story.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The opening paragraph of a short story describes a group of friends who are driving in a car to a party. The paragraph ends with the line, "The road wound ahead, a ribbon of black asphalt snaking through the moonlit pines." What is the most likely reason the writer chose to end the paragraph with that line?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    '934f304c_english_00676',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"934f304c","opensat_index":676}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice most logically completes the text? ',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Ancient Native American and Australian Aboriginal cultures described the Pleiades, which was referred to in Greek mythology as the Seven Sisters, as having seven stars.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Although once referred to as the Seven Sisters, the Pleiades appears to have only six stars today.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"In the time since ancient cultures described the Pleiades as having seven stars, two of the cluster’s stars have moved so close together that they now appear as one.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The Pleiades has seven stars, but two are so close together that they appear to be a single star.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'Choice C is the best answer. The sentence specifies the reason the Pleiades’ appearance changed, noting that two of the cluster’s stars have moved so close together that they now appear as one star.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'Ancient Native American and Australian Aboriginal cultures described the Pleiades star cluster as having seven stars. It was referred to as the Seven Sisters in the mythology of ancient Greece. Today, the cluster appears to have only six stars. Two of the stars have moved so close together that they now appear as one. The student wants to specify the reason the Pleiades’ appearance changed. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Craft and Structure',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Craft and Structure']::text[],
    'a86c0b1b_english_00677',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Craft and Structure","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"a86c0b1b","opensat_index":677}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice completes the passage with the most logical and precise word or phrase?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"although attending college events can contribute to a sense of belonging, it is not the only factor.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"attending college events is a necessary requirement for feeling a sense of belonging.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"a sense of belonging to the college community is a product of the number of college events attended.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"extracurricular activities are a more important factor in creating a sense of belonging than attending college events.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage suggests that attending college events can contribute to a sense of belonging, but the study found that it''s not the only factor. The study also found that students who participated in extracurricular activities reported feeling a higher sense of belonging. Therefore, the most logical and precise completion is "although attending college events can contribute to a sense of belonging, it is not the only factor."',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'A study of college students found that students who regularly attend college events, such as sporting events, concerts, or lectures, tend to have a higher sense of belonging to the college community. These students also report feeling more connected to other students and faculty. However, the study found that attending these events was not the sole reason for this sense of belonging. Students who had participated in extracurricular activities, such as clubs or volunteer organizations, also reported a higher sense of belonging to the college community. Therefore, the study’s findings suggest that ______',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'opensat_71b88ee5bb24fd15_english_00678',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_b8","opensat_index":678}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'What is the main idea of this passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Paul is struggling to adjust to his new surroundings.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Paul is exploring the town in hopes of finding something that will make him feel less alone.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Paul is feeling a mixture of envy and loneliness.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Paul feels a strange sense of connection to a forgotten place.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage is primarily about Paul''s struggles to adjust to his new surroundings.  He is feeling lonely and isolated, and he is trying to find a way to connect with his new environment.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The story is told from the perspective of a young boy named Paul.  His family has recently moved to a new town, and he is struggling to adjust to his new surroundings.  Paul’s mother constantly reassures him that everything will be fine, but Paul remains skeptical. He spends much of his time exploring the town on his own, hoping to find something that will make him feel less alone.  One afternoon, Paul finds himself wandering through a park.  The park is empty, except for a group of older children playing in the distance.  Paul watches them for a while, feeling a mixture of envy and loneliness. As he continues his walk, Paul comes across a small, abandoned building.  He peers through a broken window and sees a dusty, forgotten world inside.  The building is filled with old furniture, books, and toys.  Paul feels a strange sense of connection to this forgotten place.  He imagines the lives of the people who once lived there, and he wonders if they ever think about him.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'opensat_002f5c126fbd07b3_english_00679',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_a7","opensat_index":679}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best combines the sentences at the underlined portion to create the most effective and grammatically correct sentence? ',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"She lives with her guardian, Tante Louise, in a grand French house with a grim sleepy tante and no companions of her own age, in the story, \"Odalie\" is an 1899 short story by Alice Dunbar-Nelson, who portrays Odalie as eager to escape the monotony of her everyday life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"She lives with her guardian, Tante Louise, in a grand French house with a grim sleepy tante and no companions of her own age; in the story, \"Odalie\" is an 1899 short story by Alice Dunbar-Nelson, who portrays Odalie as eager to escape the monotony of her everyday life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"She lives with her guardian, Tante Louise, in a grand French house with a grim sleepy tante and no companions of her own age, and in the story, \"Odalie\" is an 1899 short story by Alice Dunbar-Nelson, who portrays Odalie as eager to escape the monotony of her everyday life.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"She lives with her guardian, Tante Louise, in a grand French house with a grim sleepy tante and no companions of her own age, but in the story, \"Odalie\" is an 1899 short story by Alice Dunbar-Nelson, who portrays Odalie as eager to escape the monotony of her everyday life.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'Choice C is the best option to combine the sentences at the underlined portion. It uses the conjunction "and" to connect the two independent clauses, creating a grammatically correct sentence that flows smoothly. The other choices contain errors in punctuation and/or conjunction use.  Choice A uses a comma to connect two independent clauses, creating a comma splice.  Choice B uses a semicolon to connect two independent clauses, which creates a semicolon splice.  Choice D uses a comma followed by the conjunction "but" to connect two independent clauses, which creates a comma splice.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The novel’s protagonist, a young woman named Odalie, is eager to escape the monotony of her everyday life. She lives with her guardian, Tante Louise, in a grand French house with a grim sleepy tante and no companions of her own age. In the story, "Odalie" is an 1899 short story by Alice Dunbar-Nelson, who portrays Odalie as eager to escape the monotony of her everyday life. Life in the French house, for Odalie, is a “dull thing.”  She is “ready for any new sensation.”  In the old French house on Royal Street, with its quaint windows and Spanish courtyard green and cool, and made musical by the plashing of the fountain and the trill of caged birds, lived Odalie in convent-like seclusion.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'opensat_4c974369be47ba77_english_00680',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_a1","opensat_index":680}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author claims that the relationship between a person’s environment and personality is not as strong as previously thought because',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"personality is a complex construct that is shaped by multiple factors.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"research has found that people''s personalities are not as easily shaped as once thought.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"people tend to have stronger personalities than their surroundings would suggest.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"there is a lack of consensus among researchers about the relationship between environment and personality.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The passage states that the relationship between environment and personality is not as strong as previously thought.  The reason given is that "a person''s personality is a complex construct and is shaped by multiple factors." This suggests that the relationship is weaker because personality is influenced by more than just the environment.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The article claims that, despite a pervasive understanding that a person’s environment shapes their personality, recent research has found that the relationship between a person’s environment and their personality is not as strong as it was previously thought. This is due to a number of factors, including the fact that a person’s personality is a complex construct and is shaped by multiple factors.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'opensat_74d71cfa703e0ec3_english_00681',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_a1","opensat_index":681}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The main point of this paragraph is that a good introduction should',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"provide a brief overview of the topic being discussed.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"establish the writer’s credibility and authority.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"captivate the reader and inspire them to continue reading.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"clearly state the writer’s main thesis statement.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The paragraph discusses the importance of an introduction grabbing the reader’s attention and making them want to read on. The techniques mentioned in the paragraph are ways of achieving that goal, so the main point is to captivate the reader.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The purpose of a good introduction is to grab the reader’s attention and make them want to read on. There are several techniques that writers can use to achieve this goal, such as starting with a surprising or thought-provoking statement, using vivid imagery, or asking a question. What does this paragraph most directly explain?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    'opensat_f2dba6c0593b821c_english_00682',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_a4","opensat_index":682}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following revisions of the sentence above is the most effective?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The American Revolution was a complex period in which many changes occurred—for example, the formation of a new government and the adoption of a new constitution.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The American Revolution was a complex period, in which many changes occurred, such as the formation of a new government and the adoption of a new constitution.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The American Revolution was a complex period, with many changes, including the formation of a new government and the adoption of a new constitution.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The American Revolution was a complex period, because many changes occurred, such as the formation of a new government and the adoption of a new constitution.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'Choice A is the most effective revision of the sentence. It uses the correct punctuation for an appositive phrase, which is a phrase that renames a noun or pronoun. The other choices either use incorrect punctuation or make the sentence unnecessarily wordy.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'A group of students are preparing a presentation on the history of the United States. They want to include a section on the American Revolution, and they’ve written the following sentence in their script: “The American Revolution was a complex period in which many changes occurred, such as the formation of a new government and the adoption of a new constitution.”',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'opensat_f62574302479576c_english_00683',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_e3","opensat_index":683}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best states the main idea of the text?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"There was a distinct difference between the artist’s work in the 1960s and in the 1970s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The artist’s work was highly influential among other artists, and it had a significant impact on the art world.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The artist’s work was often used as a teaching tool for other artists.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The artist was a talented and innovative artist, but their work was not appreciated until after their death.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The text is mainly about the influence of the artist on the art world. The text states that the artist’s work was “highly influential” among other artists, so that is the main point the author is making about the artist’s work. Therefore, the best answer is choice B.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The text provides a brief summary of what the author is trying to convey to the reader. The author is writing about an American artist who worked in the 1960s and 1970s. The artist''s work, in particular, was highly influential among other artists, and this is the main point that the author is trying to make about this artist’s work. Which choice best states the main idea of the text?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    'opensat_b9908d6ecdd5e573_english_00684',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_a5","opensat_index":684}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best revises the underlined portion of the sentence to make it consistent with the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"It was built by ancient Egyptians, and the Sphinx is said to have been carved from a single block of limestone.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"It was built by ancient Egyptians, and it is said to have been carved from a single block of limestone.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"It was built by ancient Egyptians, the Sphinx is said to have been carved from a single block of limestone.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"It was built by ancient Egyptians, the Sphinx is said to have been carved from a single block of limestone, and the Sphinx is","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The underlined portion of the sentence is a comma splice, which is a grammatical error.  Choice B corrects the error by adding a comma and the pronoun "it" to make the sentence grammatically correct.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The Great Sphinx of Giza is a monumental statue, one of the most famous landmarks in the world. It was built by ancient Egyptians, and the Sphinx is said to have been carved from a single block of limestone. The Sphinx is often depicted with a human head and a lion’s body.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Standard English Conventions']::text[],
    '63f12476_english_00685',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Standard English Conventions","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"63f12476","opensat_index":685}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author cites the example of teenagers texting each other while sitting in the same room to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"argue that teenagers today are more likely to use technology to communicate than to interact in person.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"illustrate that the author believes digital technology can have a negative impact on social interactions.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"suggest that teenagers may be using technology to avoid face-to-face interactions.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"describe the growing tendency among teenagers to use technology for communication purposes.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The author cites the example of teenagers texting each other while sitting in the same room to show that teenagers may be using technology to avoid face-to-face interactions, which can have a negative impact on social interactions. The author is not arguing that teenagers today are more likely to use technology to communicate than to interact in person, nor is the author suggesting that teenagers may be using technology to avoid face-to-face interactions. The author is simply describing the growing tendency among teenagers to use technology for communication purposes.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author discusses the impact of digital technology on the social lives of teenagers. The author asserts that, in recent years, there has been a growing tendency among teenagers to use technology as a way of maintaining friendships, even when they are physically together. For example, the author describes how some teenagers will text each other even when they are sitting in the same room. In contrast, the author notes that, in earlier generations, teenagers were more likely to engage in face-to-face interactions, even when they were communicating with friends from a distance. The author concludes that, although digital technology can facilitate social connections, it can also have a negative impact on the quality of those connections. What is the most likely reason why the author cites the example of teenagers texting each other while sitting in the same room?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Information and Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',
    true,
    ARRAY['college_board','opensat','english','Information and Ideas']::text[],
    '8a77a4b5_english_00686',
    'college_board_opensat',
    '{"opensat_domain":"Information and Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"8a77a4b5","opensat_index":686}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice provides the best way to combine the first and second sentences in the passage?  The first novel in the _Left Behind_ series, _Left Behind_, was published in 1995 by Tim LaHaye and Jerry B. Jenkins, and it was part of a trend of apocalyptic fiction that became popular in the late 1990s.',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The first novel in the _Left Behind_ series, _Left Behind_, was published in 1995 by Tim LaHaye and Jerry B. Jenkins, which was part of a trend of apocalyptic fiction that became popular in the late 1990s.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The first novel in the _Left Behind_ series, _Left Behind_, was published in 1995 by Tim LaHaye and Jerry B. Jenkins; it was part of a trend of apocalyptic fiction that became popular in the late 1990s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The first novel in the _Left Behind_ series, _Left Behind_, was published in 1995 by Tim LaHaye and Jerry B. Jenkins, it was part of a trend of apocalyptic fiction that became popular in the late 1990s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The first novel in the _Left Behind_ series, _Left Behind_, was published in 1995 by Tim LaHaye and Jerry B. Jenkins, and it is part of a trend of apocalyptic fiction that became popular in the late 1990s.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'Choice A is the best way to combine the sentences because the phrase "which was" correctly indicates that the second sentence is providing additional information about the first sentence.  The other choices are incorrect because they do not use appropriate punctuation or conjunctions to link the two sentences.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The first novel in the _Left Behind_ series, _Left Behind_, was published in 1995 by Tim LaHaye and Jerry B. Jenkins. It was part of a trend of apocalyptic fiction that became popular in the late 1990s. _Left Behind_ was later followed by a series of sequels, which were written by Jenkins alone, but LaHaye remained involved in the series as a consultant.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    NULL,

This, however, is not entirely true. While the pandemic and its aftermath may have played a role; the \"Great Resignation\" was also fueled by a growing sense of dissatisfaction among workers, who felt that they were being taken advantage of.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Many people believe that the \"Great Resignation,\" which began during the COVID-19 pandemic, was caused by the pandemic and its aftermath. While the pandemic and its aftermath may have played a role, the \"Great Resignation\" was also fueled by a growing sense of dissatisfaction among workers, who felt that they were being taken advantage of.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Many people believe that the \"Great Resignation\", which began during the COVID-19 pandemic, was caused by the pandemic and its aftermath. While the pandemic and its aftermath may have played a role, the \"Great Resignation\" was also fueled by a growing sense of dissatisfaction among workers, who felt that they were being taken advantage of.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Many people believe that the \"Great Resignation\", which began during the COVID-19 pandemic, was caused by the pandemic and its aftermath. This, however, is not entirely true. While the pandemic and its aftermath may have played a role, the \"Great Resignation\" was also fueled by a growing sense of dissatisfaction among workers who felt that they were being taken advantage of.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'In this passage, the shift from the traditional to the recent is indicated by the use of the phrase',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"“customary,” which suggests that the practice of using materials to create clothing is old, or traditional.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"“within the recent past,” which explicitly states that a change has occurred recently.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"“There are other elements of the passage that may help to make the speaker seem more immediate and personal,” which indicates that the change has occurred recently.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"“The speaker refers to events and places that are familiar to the reader,” which explicitly states that a change has occurred recently.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The phrase "within the recent past" is an explicit statement that the change is recent. The phrase implies that the practice of using materials to create clothing is older than the recent change being discussed. The other answer choices are incorrect. Choices A and D are incorrect because they are general statements about the passage, and Choice C is incorrect because it is not a specific statement about how the change is indicated. Choice B is the best answer because it is an explicit statement about the change that has occurred.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The first paragraph of the passage begins by describing a traditional, or customary, way of using materials to create clothing. But then it transitions to an observation about a change that has occurred within the recent past. How does the text indicate that the change is recent?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',

    'C',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Fitzgerald’s contrasting of Gatsby’s “formless grace” and “punctilious manner” is most likely intended to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"show that Gatsby''s sense of style is more important to him than his sense of social decorum.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"highlight the contradictions inherent in Gatsby’s character and personality.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"suggest that Gatsby is trying to create a false impression of himself.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"illustrate the depth of Gatsby’s social and emotional complexity.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The passage indicates that Gatsby is both “formless” and “punctilious,” which suggests that he has contradictory characteristics.  The contrast between Gatsby’s “formless grace” and “punctilious manner” highlights the internal contradictions that define his character and personality.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'In the novel *The Great Gatsby*, F. Scott Fitzgerald describes the title character, Jay Gatsby, as a man who is both “formless” and “punctilious.” Gatsby''s “formless grace” is evident in his movements and his style of dress, while his “punctilious manner” is evident in his attention to detail and his adherence to social etiquette.  What is the purpose of Fitzgerald’s contrasting these two characteristics of Gatsby?',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best combines the sentences at the underlined portion to maintain the style and flow of the passage?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"Some of the artist’s portraits feature realistic details and complex compositions; while others are more stylized, using bold colors and abstract shapes to create a mood.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"Some of the artist’s portraits feature realistic details and complex compositions; others are more stylized, using bold colors and abstract shapes to create a mood.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"Some of the artist’s portraits feature realistic details and complex compositions, others are more stylized, using bold colors and abstract shapes to create a mood.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"Some of the artist’s portraits feature realistic details and complex compositions; others are more stylized, using bold colors, and abstract shapes to create a mood.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The correct answer is B. Using a semicolon to join two independent clauses that are closely related in meaning creates a smooth transition and avoids a comma splice.  The semicolon is followed by a comma because it’s necessary to separate the introductory phrase "others are more stylized" from the rest of the sentence.  The other answer choices either make an incorrect use of punctuation or don’t maintain the style and flow of the passage.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The artist, who is a master of the art of portraiture, has several distinctive styles.  Some of the artist’s portraits feature realistic details and complex compositions, while others are more stylized, using bold colors and abstract shapes to create a mood.  The artist’s portraits are widely collected, and they are often exhibited in major museums.',
    'passage',

    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,

    false,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'What is the strongest evidence provided in this passage that supports the claim that the educational game increases middle school students'' accuracy in adding fractions?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The psychologist chose a random sample of 35 students from all of the students at one of the middle schools in a large city.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The psychologist found that students who played the game showed significant improvement in accuracy when adding fractions.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The study showed that students who played the game showed significant improvement in accuracy.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The study used a random sample of 35 students from one of the middle schools in a large city.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The text says that the "psychologist found that students who played the game showed significant improvement in accuracy when adding fractions."  This directly supports the claim that the educational game increases middle school students’ accuracy. The other choices provide background information about the study, but don’t directly support the main claim.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The text states that an educational game increases middle school students’ accuracy in adding fractions. What is the most important piece of information that supports this statement?  The psychologist chose a random sample of 35 students from all of the students at one of the middle schools in a large city. The psychologist found that students who played the game showed significant improvement in accuracy when adding fractions.',

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best corrects the error in the underlined portion?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"books were painstakingly copied by hand, making it expensive and rare.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"books were painstakingly copied by hand, making they expensive and rare.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"books were painstakingly copied by hand, making them expensive and rare, ","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"books were painstakingly copied by hand, making it expensive and rare;","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The pronoun "it" is used correctly because it refers to the noun phrase "books were painstakingly copied by hand."  The pronoun "they" is incorrect because it does not agree with the singular noun phrase. The comma after "rare" in choice C is unnecessary and creates a comma splice. The semicolon in choice D is incorrect because the two clauses before and after the semicolon are not closely related.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The invention of the printing press in the 15th century revolutionized the way information was disseminated. Before, books were painstakingly copied by hand, making them expensive and rare. But with the printing press, books could be reproduced quickly and cheaply, making them more accessible to a wider audience. Printing also played a key role in the spread of literacy.',

    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The author uses the example of the traveler passing through a forest to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"illustrate the idea that experiencing nature requires more than simply observing it.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"argue that the traveler is not truly experiencing nature because they are not spending enough time in the forest.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"suggest that the forest is a place of great beauty and wonder.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"compare the traveler’s experience of the forest to the experiences of others.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The author is using the example of the traveler to make a distinction between simply seeing something and truly experiencing it. The example suggests that experiencing nature requires more than simply observing it; it requires a deeper level of engagement with the environment.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author''s argument is based on the idea that there is a difference between being in nature and experiencing nature. For example, a traveler passing through a forest may see the trees and the animals, but they may not truly experience the forest. To experience the forest, the traveler must engage with it on a deeper level. This can be done by spending time in the forest, observing the details of the environment, and reflecting on the meaning of the forest.  What is the most likely reason why the author uses this example?',
    'passage',

    'Expression of Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'The comma after “smaller” is used to',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"separate two independent clauses in a compound sentence.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"set off a nonrestrictive adjective clause.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"indicate the end of a dependent clause.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"separate two words in a series.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'C',
    'The clause “but now I just try to be me” is a dependent clause, which means that it relies on the independent clause “I used to try to make myself smaller” to be a complete sentence.  The comma after “smaller” indicates the end of the dependent clause.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The following text is adapted from a novel about a young woman named Lena who struggles with being perceived as different from others.  "I used to try to make myself smaller," Lena says, "but now I just try to be me."  The text goes on to describe Lena’s realization that she doesn’t need to change herself to fit in with others.  What is the most likely reason the author uses a comma after “smaller”?',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',

    'SAT'::exam_type,

(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which of the following best describes the author''s use of the word "you" in the sentence "you will find that the style of your writing can be more effective, but it may also be more difficult to read"?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"The author is attempting to persuade the reader to accept his point of view.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"The author is attempting to create a sense of intimacy and connection with the reader.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"The author is attempting to emphasize the importance of the speaker’s message.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"The author is attempting to suggest that the speaker is addressing a large audience.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'The author is attempting to create a sense of intimacy and connection with the reader by speaking directly to the reader, as if they are having a personal conversation. This helps to make the reader feel more involved in the process of writing, and it can help to make the author''s message more persuasive.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The author''s central idea is to show that the use of a simple, straightforward style is not always the most effective way to write. The author uses a variety of rhetorical devices, including metaphors, similes, and personification, to make his point. The author’s use of the word “you,” as in the sentence “you will find that the style of your writing can be more effective, but it may also be more difficult to read,” is a deliberate choice to make the reader feel more involved in the process of writing. This is because, by speaking directly to the reader in a way that feels more personal and intimate, the writer is able to create a greater sense of connection with the reader.  The passage, however, lacks a conclusion.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,
    false,
    'reading_writing',
    'Expression of Ideas',
    NULL,
    'college_board_sat_v1',

    '{"opensat_domain":"Expression of Ideas","section":"english","opensat_raw_id":"random_id_22","opensat_index":697}'::jsonb
  ),
(
    gen_random_uuid(),
    'Which choice best combines the sentences at the underlined portion in a way that conforms to the conventions of Standard English?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"the researcher’s work suggests that these insects are beneficial, however, the researcher’s work does not mean that all insects are beneficial, and some may even be harmful to certain plants and animals in the environment.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"the researcher’s work suggests that these insects are beneficial, but the researcher’s work does not mean that all insects are beneficial, and some may even be harmful to certain plants and animals in the environment.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"the researcher’s work suggests that these insects are beneficial; however, the researcher’s work does not mean that all insects are beneficial, and some may even be harmful to certain plants and animals in the environment.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"the researcher’s work suggests that these insects are beneficial, and the researcher’s work does not mean that all insects are beneficial, and some may even be harmful to certain plants and animals in the environment.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'B',
    'Choice B combines the sentences using the conjunction "but." The conjunction "but" indicates that the second clause is in contrast to the first clause, which is the intended meaning of the sentence. The other choices create either a run-on sentence or a comma splice.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The study shows that certain kinds of insects are often found in the soil. The researcher’s work suggests that these insects are beneficial to the environment and that it’s important to conserve them. However, the researcher’s work does not mean that all insects are beneficial, and some may even be harmful to certain plants and animals in the environment.',
    'passage',
    'reading_writing',
    'Standard English Conventions',
    'medium'::difficulty,
    'SAT'::exam_type,
    true,
    false,

    'Which of the following is the most effective way to revise the underlined portion of the sentence to create a more grammatically correct and concise sentence?',
    'multiple_choice',
    '[{"id":"A","text":"the farms are also dependent on the weather for efficiency.","is_correct":true,"explanation":""},{"id":"B","text":"the farms are also dependent on the weather’s efficiency.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"C","text":"the farms are also dependent on the weather, for efficiency.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""},{"id":"D","text":"in addition to the sun’s rays, the weather also determines their efficiency.","is_correct":false,"explanation":""}]'::jsonb,
    'A',
    'The original sentence is grammatically incorrect because it includes a comma splice.  The comma splice can be corrected by replacing the comma with a semicolon, as in choice A.  The other choices are incorrect because they don’t effectively revise the sentence or because they create new grammatical errors.',
    'Review the passage and eliminate choices that contradict the text.',
    'The article discusses a new method for producing energy: solar farms.  The farms are made of solar panels which can convert sunlight into electricity.  The article states, "In addition to the sun’s rays, the farms are also dependent on the weather for efficiency."',
